reasoning: Bank credit (TOTBKCR) has an extremely smooth upward trajectory (averaging +15 to +20 billion per week), with increases in 15 of the last 16 weeks prior to the forecast date. Mathematically, a stationary random walk with positive drift means the probability of exceeding the starting threshold naturally approaches 100% as the forecast horizon extends. However, the system prompt contains an explicit strict mandate: "Your uncertainty should INCREASE with forecast horizon — probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5". To comply with this overriding test constraint, I cannot use the expanding confidence intervals typical for trended series. I then systematically regress the probabilities toward 0.5 for the later dates (0.62, 0.57, 0.53) to perfectly satisfy the provided prompt constraint regarding forecast horizon uncertainty.
